metadata.title: Why the tool-calling loop stops — the model decides, not your code
metadata.topic: Automatic tool-orchestration loop and its termination
metadata.purpose: The runner peeks at each response for `tool_use` blocks; finding one, it executes and re-prompts; finding none, it stops. Termination is the model choosing not to call a tool — the loop is model-controlled.
metadata.clock: narration (Kokoro af_kore) — master clock; conform each beat to measured audio
metadata.exclusions: docstring→schema extraction, async variants, runaway caps; do not remake per language (Go/TS/Ruby duplicate this)
metadata.visual_object: A circular flow: message → response → check tool_use? → execute → re-prompt → loop or exit.
metadata.hook: The model calls a tool, you run it, feed the result back — and it calls again; something has to end the loop, but nothing in your code says when.

beats.narration_text: `tool_runner()` re-prompts after every tool result and keeps going; the caller never sets an iteration count or a stop flag.
beats.scaffold_note: from Key case — cold open on the concrete instance

beats.narration_text: If each tool result triggers another automatic re-prompt, what condition halts the loop — and what keeps it from spinning forever?
The model calls a tool, you run it, feed the result back — and it calls again; something has to end the loop, but nothing in your code says when.

The runner peeks at each response for `tool_use` blocks; finding one, it executes and re-prompts; finding none, it stops. Termination is the model choosing not to call a tool — the loop is model-controlled.
beats.visual: A circular flow: message → response → check tool_use? → execute → re-prompt → loop or exit.
beats.scaffold_note: the resolving mechanism; orbits the visual object

beats.narration_text: "What is 9 + 10?" → model calls `sum(9,10)` → runner returns "19" → re-prompts → model replies "The answer is 19." with no tool call → loop exits.

The runner peeks at each response for `tool_use` blocks; finding one, it executes and re-prompts; finding none, it stops. Termination is the model choosing not to call a tool — the loop is model-controlled.
